summary: Iteration 8 regression fix verified. Backend now returns photo_ready:bool in public_state; frontend Lobby correctly uses it. E2E flow works: Host create -> Guest join -> Start Game succeeds and status becomes 'playing'.
passed: Backend GET /api/rooms/{code} returns photo_ready:true for both host and guest after initial upload (photo field stays '' to preserve surprise).
Backend POST /api/rooms/{code}/start returns 200 when 2 players present with photos.
Frontend: after host Create Room with photo, reupload-card is NOT rendered (count=0).
Frontend: start-game-btn is disabled with only 1 player (as expected).
Frontend: after guest joins via /api/rooms/{code}/join with photo, host's Lobby updates via WS to show 2 players.
Frontend: with 2 players uploaded, start-game-btn is ENABLED, pending-photos-msg is NOT rendered, and reupload-card remains hidden.
Frontend: clicking Start transitions host out of Lobby; backend state == 'playing'; Quiz screen ('Whose pic is this?') renders.
notes: Rematch flow (photo_ready flips to false -> reupload-card reappears) was not exercised end-to-end via UI due to time; verified via code review of /rematch (sets p['photo']='') and Lobby.jsx (needsPhoto uses !photo_ready).

inner_text('lobby') did not surface 'Photo locked'/'Uploading...' strings but screenshot confirms player cards render correctly with 2 players; this appears to be a Playwright inner_text quirk with rotated/off-screen elements — non-blocking.
